data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix's architecture is based on microservices, allowing each service to be developed, deployed, and scaled independently.
data.technical_spec: Services communicate over RESTful APIs and are deployed on a scalable AWS cloud infrastructure.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network
data.description: Netflix uses a broad network of data centers and CDNs to deliver content efficiently around the globe.
data.technical_spec: Data is cached at edge locations close to users, using AWS CloudFront and proprietary solutions.

data.title: Chaos Engineering
data.description: The approach of intentionally introducing failures to test the robustness of the system.
data.technical_spec: Tools like Chaos Monkey randomly disable servers to test resiliency.

data.title: Open Connect
data.description: An in-house content delivery network designed to reduce bandwidth costs and improve streaming quality.
data.technical_spec: Uses physical appliances placed within ISPs to store content.

data.title: API Gateway
data.description: Used to manage and route API requests between clients and backend services.
data.technical_spec: Custom-built API gateway that handles millions of requests, developed for high availability.
